sections.paragraph:
ἐκπνέω (s. πνέω) 1 aor. ἐξέπνευσα breathe out one’s life/soul, expire, euphem. for die Mk 15:37, 39; Lk 23:46 (without obj. since Soph., Aj. 1026; Plut., Mor. 597f; M. Ant. 4, 33, 2; Jos., Ant. 12, 357; PHolm ι [10],18. W. βίον or ψυχήν since Aeschyl.).—TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

euphem. euphem. = euphemism

obj. obj. = object, objective

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PHolm PHolm = Papyrus Graecus Holmiensis—List 4

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 308
